title: Function pointers should not be converted to any other type
description: Function pointers should not be converted to any other typeAnalyze your codeintentionality-logicalreliabilityBugbased-on-misraWhy is this an issue?More InfoConversion of a function pointer to a different type of pointer results in undefined behaviour. This means, for example, that a pointer to a function cannot be converted to a pointer to a different type of function.Noncompliant code exampleint f(int a) { float (*p)(float) = (float (*)(float)) & f; // Noncompliant }Available In:Catch issues on the fly,in your IDEDetect issues in your GitHub, Azure DevOps Services, Bitbucket Cloud, GitLab repositoriesAnalyze code in youron-premise CIDeveloper EditionAvailable Since9.1©2025SonarSource Sàrl. recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Function pointers should not be converted to any other type. Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.
